questions.html: Which line starts a loop that runs exactly 4 times?
questions.notice: for i in range(4): — don't forget the colon at the end!

questions.html: Which line starts a loop that runs exactly 10 times?
questions.notice: for i in range(10): — range(10) gives 0 to 9, which is 10 numbers

questions.html: Which line starts a loop where i goes from 1 to 5?
questions.notice: for i in range(1, 6): — to include 5, the stop value must be 6

questions.html: Which line starts a loop where i goes from 0 to 9?
questions.notice: for i in range(10): — range(10) starts at 0 and stops before 10

questions.html: Which line starts a loop where i goes from 3 to 7?
questions.notice: for i in range(3, 8): — starts at 3 and stops before 8, so 3, 4, 5, 6, 7

questions.html: Which line starts a loop that runs exactly once?
questions.notice: for i in range(1): — range(1) produces only the number 0, so the loop runs once

questions.html: Which line starts a loop where i goes from 5 to 9?
questions.notice: for i in range(5, 10): — starts at 5 and stops before 10, so 5, 6, 7, 8, 9

questions.html: Which line starts a loop that runs exactly 3 times?
questions.notice: for i in range(3): — produces 0, 1, 2 which is 3 numbers

questions.html: Which line starts a loop where i goes from 0 to 4?
questions.notice: for i in range(5): — range(5) produces 0, 1, 2, 3, 4

questions.html: Which of these is a valid first line of a for loop?
questions.notice: for i in range(1, 8): — needs 'in', needs brackets, and needs a colon at the end
